title: Harden the redundancy guard for precision (review-swarm findings)
context: A 21-agent adversarial review of the redundancy guard (16/17 findings confirmed) verified the safety/invariant story (advisory-only, read-only, never blocks) but surfaced real precision false-positives. The non-obvious one: `hunch` lists changed files with `git diff --diff-filter=ACMR`, which EXCLUDES the Delete side of a move. So a sub-threshold move+edit (Add new path + Delete old path, git doesn't detect the rename) leaves the old definition in the graph and NOT in the changed-file set — every carried-over top-level symbol was falsely flagged as 'already defined elsewhere' on a routine refactor.
decision: Three precision fixes, all preserving the advisory-only + read-only contract: (1) MOVE-AWARE — redundantSymbols now also excludes existing symbols whose file is in the diff's filesRenamed.from or filesDeleted set, and skips any added name that also appears in removedSymbols (moved, not duplicated); buildCheckReport threads these from the already-computed DiffAnalysis. (2) ROOT-SCOPED — only match existing symbols sharing a top-level root with the changed files, so a src/ change never collides with test/ or the vscode-extension/ sub-project (the indexer's SKIP_DIRS does not exclude those). (3) VETO-LEAK — `hunch veto` (vetoOnly) now zeroes `redundant` too, so the isolated veto view shows only reversals.
consequences: Move+edit refactors no longer produce phantom redundancy noise — the most common real-world false positive.

Graph staleness (hunch check does not re-index) and intentional same-named helpers across modules remain known, accepted limitations of an advisory heuristic; mitigated by stopwords + length floor + root-scoping, not eliminated.
EXISTING_KINDS keeps 'variable' for forward-compat though the current indexer emits arrow-fn consts as 'function' (so const re-implementations are still caught); documented to avoid future confusion.
alternatives_rejected: Changing redundantSymbols to take the full DiffAnalysis as its first arg — would churn the existing unit-test call sites; used an optional opts param instead (back-compatible).
Hardcoding test/vscode-extension/site exclusions — root-scoping by the changed files' own top-level dirs is general and needs no repo-specific list.
